document.title: Attachment 5 CBTC/TR for power supply box, type DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A
document.documentType: CB Test Certificate and IEC 60601-1 test report
authorityOrJurisdiction: National Certification Body, Gaustadalléen 30, NO-0373 Oslo, Norway
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No. 352278; Amendment No. 2; Ref. Certif. No. NO82590/A1/M2 and NO96755/M1
document.documentDate: Date of issue: 2014-09-18, Amendment No. 2: 2018-04-23; CB certificates dated 24-04-2018

sourceSummary.oneLine: IECEE CB certificates and Nemko IEC 60601-1 test reports cover DAEAN ELECOM power supply models DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports safety conformity evidence for a component power supply used in an end product, with stated limitations requiring end-product evaluation.
keyPoints: A sample of each power supply model was tested and found in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.
The report identifies the product as a component power supply and states intended use shall be checked in the end product.
Risk management according to ISO 14971:2007 was not covered and must be evaluated in the end product.
DEPS9601A is identified as a variant of DEPS9601 with different model designation and input rating.
The equipment was evaluated as Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment, and not intended for patient connection.

evidence.claim: The document covers power supply models DEPS9601 and DEPS9601A.
The test item is a power supply.
The CB certificates state conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005 and IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: A sample of the product was tested and found to be in conformity with IEC 60601-1:2005, IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012

evidence.claim: Report No. 352278 is dated 2014-09-18 with Amendment No. 2 dated 2018-04-23.
evidence.evidenceText: Report Number: 352278; Date of issue: 2014-09-18, Amendment No. 2: 2018-04-23

evidence.claim: The 2018 CB certificates replace earlier certificates due to technical modification.
evidence.evidenceText: This certificate replaces the certificate NO82590/A1/M1, due to technical modification. This certificate replaces the certificate NO96755, due to technical modification.

evidence.claim: The applicant, manufacturer, and factory are DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.
evidence.evidenceText: Name and address of the applicant ... DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.; Name and address of the manufacturer ... DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.; Name and address of the factory ... DAEAN ELECOM CO., LTD.

evidence.claim: DEPS9601 is rated 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Class I, with 3 A, 16 V output.
evidence.evidenceText: For model DEPS9601 Input: 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Cl. I Output: 3 A, 16 V

evidence.claim: DEPS9601A is rated for AC input or 24 Vdc input and outputs 3 A, 16 V.
evidence.evidenceText: For model DEPS9601A Input: 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Cl. I or 3 A, 24 V Output: 3 A, 16 V

evidence.claim: DEPS9601A is identical to DEPS9601 except for model type designation and input rating.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant mode DEPS9601A is identical to basic model DEPS9601 except for model type designation and input rating.

evidence.claim: The equipment is a component and its intended use must be checked in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Device type (component/sub-assembly/ equipment/ system): Component; Intended use ... Shall be checked in the end product.

evidence.claim: The equipment was evaluated as Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment, and not intended for patient connection.
evidence.evidenceText: Equipment has been evaluated as Class I, continuous operation, ordinary equipment ... The equipment is not intended for patient connection.

evidence.claim: Risk management according to ISO 14971:2007 is excluded from the report and must be evaluated in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk management process according to ISO 14971:2007 is not covered in this report and must be evaluated in the end product.

evidence.claim: The report states the product fulfils IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of - IEC 60601-1:2005 + AMD1:2012 and EN 60601-1:2006 + A1:2013

evidence.claim: Report No. 352278 must be used with main test report No. 269183 and amendment test report No. 323575.
evidence.evidenceText: This amendment test report No. 352278 shall always be used together with the main test report No. 269183, 323575.

evidence.claim: The 2018 amendment concerned addition of protection circuitry in the secondary circuit.
evidence.evidenceText: The updates concerned in this test report are as follows; - Addition of protection circuitry in secondary circuit.

evidence.claim: Leakage current tests passed for the evaluated models.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.7 LEAKAGE CURRENTS and PATIENT AUXILIARY CURRENTS P ... See appended table 8.7. P

evidence.claim: Dielectric strength testing passed with no dielectric breakdown in the listed MOOP insulation areas.
evidence.evidenceText: 8.8.3 TABLE: Dielectric strength test ... P ... Dielectric breakdown after 1 minute ... No

evidence.claim: Single fault condition tests in Report No. 352278 did not identify hazardous situations.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.2 TABLE: SINGLE FAULT CONDITIONS ... HAZARDOUS SITUATION (Yes/No) ... No

evidence.claim: The power supply was intended for connection to Samsung X-Ray detector type SDR-G4335W.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment is intended for connection to the X-Ray detector, type SDR-G4335W manufactured by Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.

reviewFlags.reason: Source text is a combined OCR/preprocessed document containing multiple certificates and reports from 2014, 2017, and 2018; document date and revision fields contain multiple valid dates.
The source path and metadata signal FDA, but the document text itself is an IECEE CB Scheme/Nemko test certificate and test report, not an FDA-issued document.
Some OCR artifacts and typographical errors are present, including garbled heading text and misspellings such as 'variant mode' and 'Finla input current'.
Risk management, patient connection, EMC, biocompatibility, power cord set, and several end-product installation items are explicitly deferred to end-product evaluation.
